name: Justin Bieber
description:
'''Justin Drew Bieber''' ({{IPAc-en|ˈ|b|iː|b|ər}} {{Respell|BEE|bər}}; born March 1, 1994) is a Canadian singer. Regarded as a prominent figure in contemporary [[popular music]], he rose to fame in the late 2000s with his debut [[extended play]], ''[[My World (Justin Bieber EP)|My World]]'' (2009), receiving international recognition and establishing himself as a [[teen idol]].

Bieber's debut album, ''[[My World 2.0]]'' (2010), topped the US [[Billboard 200|''Billboard'' 200]], making him the youngest solo male to do so in 47 years. Its lead single, "[[Baby (Justin Bieber song)|Baby]]", became one of the [[List of best-selling singles in the United States|best selling singles]] in the United States, while its accompanying music video became [[List of most-disliked YouTube videos|the most disliked video on YouTube]]. His second album, ''[[Under the Mistletoe]]'' (2011), became the first [[Christmas album]] by a male artist to debut atop the chart. With his third album, ''[[Believe (Justin Bieber album)|Believe]]'' (2012), and [[Believe Acoustic|its acoustic re-release]], Bieber became the first artist in ''[[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]]'''s history to have five US number-one albums by the age of 18. Following a series of controversies, he released the [[Electronic dance music|EDM]]-infused 2015 single "[[Where Are Ü Now]]", marking a departure from his teen idol image.

Bieber's fourth album, ''[[Purpose (Justin Bieber album)|Purpose]]'' (2015), yielded the US [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]] number-ones "[[Love Yourself]]", "[[Sorry (Justin Bieber song)|Sorry]]", and "[[What Do You Mean?]]". The songs monopolized the top three of the [[UK singles chart]], making Bieber the [[UK Singles Chart records and statistics#Acts to occupy the top two|first person to do so]]. In 2017, he featured on the US number-ones "[[I'm the One (DJ Khaled song)|I'm the One]]" and "[[Despacito (Justin Bieber song)|Despacito]]"; the latter tied the then-record for the [[List of Billboard Hot 100 chart achievements and milestones#Most weeks at number one|longest-running number-one song in history]] (16 weeks). With the albums ''[[Changes (Justin Bieber album)|Changes]]'' (2020) and ''[[Justice (Justin Bieber album)|Justice]]'' (2021), Bieber became the youngest solo act to have eight US number-one albums. Also in 2021, he scored his seventh and eighth US number-one singles, "[[Peaches (Justin Bieber song)|Peaches]]" and "[[Stay (The Kid Laroi and Justin Bieber song)|Stay]]". His following albums, ''[[Swag (Justin Bieber album)|Swag]]'' and ''[[Swag II]]'', were released in 2025.

Bieber is one of the [[List of best-selling music artists|best-selling music artists]] of all time, with over 150 million units sold worldwide and five [[Diamond Certified|diamond]] certifications from the [[Recording Industry Association of America]] (RIAA). [[List of awards and nominations received by Justin Bieber|His accolades]] include two [[Grammy Awards]], one [[Latin Grammy Award]], eight [[Juno Awards]], two [[Brit Awards]], 26 [[Billboard Music Award|''Billboard'' Music Awards]], 18 [[American Music Awards]], six [[MTV Video Music Awards]] and [[MTV Europe Music Award#Most nominated and winning artists|a record]] 22 [[MTV Europe Music Awards]]. ''[[Time (magazine)|Time]]'' named him one of the [[Time 100|100 most influential people in the world]] in 2011, and ''[[Forbes]]'' listed him among the top ten [[Forbes Celebrity 100|most powerful celebrities]] from 2011 to 2013. ''Billboard'' ranked him the [[Billboard's Greatest Pop Stars of the 21st Century|eighth-greatest pop star of the 21st century]].
